rationale: Okada (2005) is not merely listed as background; the citing paper summarizes its specific dessert-choice finding and interpretation, then uses it to motivate hypotheses about anticipated guilt under low-fat labels. The citation also supports the framework claim that guilt differs across hedonic versus utilitarian foods, which is carried into the empirical design. This gives the cited paper a clear conceptual role in mechanism framing rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Okada (2005)'s justification/guilt insight from hedonic versus utilitarian food choice to motivate its own mechanism that low-fat claims reduce guilt and increase intake. It applies that logic in a new setting focused on nutrition labeling, serving-size inferences, and consumption volume.
